hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Marketing: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Marketing specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Consumer Behaviour, Brand Management, Sales and Distribution, Integrated Marketing Communications, and Services Marketing in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Marketing: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. Marketing depth with Consumer Behaviour, Brand Management, Sales and Distribution, Integrated Marketing Communications, Retail Management, and Services Marketing. Strong for brand management, sales, and FMCG marketing careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Marketing is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Marketing specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Consumer Behaviour, Brand Management, Sales and Distribution Management, Integrated Marketing Communications, Retail Management, and Services Marketing. These subjects cover the full marketing function from brand strategy to channel execution.

LPU's large alumni base across FMCG, retail, and IT services companies in North India provides placement network support. The zero-cost EMI structure makes this programme accessible to working marketing professionals targeting a career upgrade without upfront fee burden.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse buying behaviour models, attitude formation, cultural influences, and purchase decision processes for marketing strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Brand Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build brand equity frameworks, manage brand architecture, and design brand revitalisation strategies for FMCG and services.

sem3Subjects.name: Sales and Distribution Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design territory-based sales organisations, manage channel partner networks, and build sales incentive structures.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Develop competitive positioning, market entry strategy, and growth frameworks for marketing-led businesses.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Marketing specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present summer training project findings to faculty panel, developing professional presentation and analytical skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied marketing project on brand strategy, new product launch, or channel optimisation.

sem4Subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communications
sem4Subjects.description: Design integrated campaign briefs across ATL, BTL, digital, and PR channels with unified brand messaging.

sem4Subjects.name: Retail Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage retail formats, visual merchandising, category management, and omnichannel retail strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Services Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Apply the 7Ps of services marketing, manage service quality, and design customer experience strategies for service businesses.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Marketing specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Marketing MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FMCG, Retail and e-commerce, IT services and SaaS, Real estate, Advertising and media, Insurance and BFSI marketing

**Entry-level roles:** Brand Executive, Sales Executive, Marketing Research Analyst, Digital Marketing Executive, Channel Sales Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Sales Manager, Marketing Manager, Trade Marketing Manager, Category Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, Head of Brand, National Sales Head, Chief Marketing Officer, Business Head

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, ITC, Nestle, P&G, Marico, Dabur, Amazon, Flipkart, Reliance Retail, Shoppers Stop, Big Bazaar, Zomato, Swiggy, HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential

skills.body:
The LPU Marketing specialisation builds skills that FMCG and retail marketing employers test at brand manager and sales manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Brand Management: brand equity measurement, brand architecture design, and brand revitalisation strategy for FMCG and services brand roles
- Consumer Behaviour: psychographic segmentation, attitude formation analysis, and cultural behaviour mapping for insights and planning roles
- Integrated Marketing Communications: campaign brief writing, media mix planning, and cross-channel message consistency for marketing communications roles
- Sales and Distribution Management: territory design, channel partner management, and sales incentive structure for field sales and distribution roles

**Applied skills:**
- Retail Management: category management, visual merchandising, and omnichannel retail strategy for retail and e-commerce management roles
- Services Marketing: service quality measurement, customer experience design, and SERVQUAL application for hospitality and financial services marketing

**Soft skills:** Presenting brand strategy to senior management, negotiating trade terms with retailer buyers, leading cross-functional new product launches, and managing advertising agency relationships.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Marketing against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Marketing:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Marketing is a strong all-India brand, particularly recognised in Delhi-NCR FMCG and services companies. LPU Marketing at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is more affordable. LPU offers zero-cost EMI; Amity does not. For North India marketing careers on a tighter budget, LPU Marketing offers a meaningful fee advantage.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Marketing:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000 with Coursera Plus integration. MUJ Marketing offers global certification content via Coursera. LPU Marketing covers Consumer Behaviour, Brand Management, Retail Management, and Services Marketing as dedicated subjects. Both are comparable options at similar fee levels.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management:** NMIMS carries strong Mumbai brand recognition in FMCG and media marketing. NMIMS Marketing Management is Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000. For Mumbai FMCG and advertising roles, NMIMS carries stronger brand recognition. For North India retail, FMCG, and sales management roles, LPU Marketing at comparable fees is a strong alternative.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Marketing offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Marketing specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Marketing alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Simran Kaur
items.liked: Brand Management and Consumer Behaviour subjects were directly applicable to my FMCG brand executive role. Zero-cost EMI made the programme accessible without disrupting my savings.
items.disliked: Retail Management cases focused more on offline formats; more e-commerce cases would be relevant.
items.body: I had 3 years in FMCG sales and the LPU Marketing MBA helped me transition to brand management. The Rs 1.61L fee with zero-cost EMI was the deciding factor over more expensive alternatives. LPU brand is well recognised at North India FMCG companies.

items.name: Mohit Sharma
items.liked: Integrated Marketing Communications and Sales and Distribution Management were well-structured. Strong practical orientation toward field sales and distribution roles.
items.disliked: Peer group was smaller in live marketing sessions compared to finance and HR. Placement portal had limited premium brand listings.
items.body: Good programme for sales and marketing careers. Delhi-NCR FMCG companies recognised the LPU credential. For pure brand strategy roles at top-tier FMCG, Amity or NMIMS would carry slightly stronger Delhi recognition. For sales management and distribution, LPU Marketing is well-positioned.

items.name: Neha Joshi
items.liked: Services Marketing subject was uniquely applicable to my hospitality marketing role. Consumer Behaviour gave me frameworks to structure market research.
items.disliked: Digital marketing content was covered in core subjects rather than as a dedicated Sem 3-4 subject.
items.body: Solid marketing credential at a very competitive price. The zero-cost EMI was the key benefit. For Rajasthan and North India marketing careers, LPU is a recognised and affordable choice.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Does LPU Online MBA Marketing cover digital marketing?
faqs.answer: Digital marketing concepts are covered in core Sem 1-2 subjects. The Marketing specialisation focuses on brand management, consumer behaviour, integrated marketing communications, retail, and services marketing in Sem 3-4. Students specifically targeting digital marketing as a primary focus should consider LPU's dedicated Digital Marketing specialisation.

faqs.question: Is LPU Marketing MBA good for FMCG brand roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. Brand Management, Consumer Behaviour, and Integrated Marketing Communications are directly applicable to FMCG brand management roles. LPU's alumni network in North India FMCG companies supports placement for Chandigarh, Delhi-NCR, and Punjab-based FMCG careers.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month for affordable monthly outflow.
You are targeting FMCG, retail, sales management, or brand management careers in North India.
consider: If Mumbai FMCG or advertising brand recognition matters, NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management carries stronger recognition in those markets.
If pan-India FMCG brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Marketing at Rs 2.25L offers strong all-India hiring recognition.
If digital marketing is the primary focus, consider LPU's Digital Marketing specialisation or MUJ Online MBA Marketing with its Coursera digital marketing modules.
